explanations.4: This checks if the fzf command is available on the system; if not, the script stops here.
explanations.15: The backslash at the end tells the shell to continue this command on the next line—it's just splitting a long line for readability.
explanations.17: The awk command splits each line at the colon and grabs just the first part, so 'dex: Exec...' becomes 'dex'.
explanations.19: This checks if the user cancelled the menu (selected_op is empty) and exits early if they did.
explanations.22: The 'type' command verifies that the function actually exists and is loaded before we try to run it, preventing errors.
